title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs unicode normalization, case folding, and original evidence from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned reversible Unicode policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Unicode Version Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 2687 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Unicode Version Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Unicode Version Evidence through the concrete versioned reversible Unicode policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Unicode Version Evidence, keep Unicode Version Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Unicode Version Evidence, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Unicode Version Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Unicode Version Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 2687 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Unicode Version Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Unicode behavior is timeless and cannot alter a report between Python releases.
